message:
chore: Update beads - environment setup complete

Closed beads:
- bd-1jt.2.5: Install and configure act on Linux host (trj)
- bd-1jt.2.3: Setup macOS build environment on mmini
- bd-1jt.2.7: Setup Windows build environment on wlap (partial - Go works, Rust needs MSVC)
- bd-1jt.2.2: Setup Linux build environment on trj

Environment status:
- trj: act 0.2.84, Rust 1.95.0-nightly (8 targets), Go 1.24.4, Zig 0.14.1, MinGW
- mmini: Rust 1.93.0-nightly, Go 1.25.6, Bun 1.3.8, universal binary support
- wlap: Go 1.25.6, Bun 1.3.8 (Rust needs MSVC Build Tools)

In progress: bd-1jt.2.1 (Implement dsr build command)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-20l","title":"E2E: dsr fallback (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr fallback (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate the end-to-end fallback flow using real build and release paths when dependencies are available.

## Approach
- Use a minimal tool config and local repo fixture.
- If act/docker/gh auth missing, skip with explicit guidance.
- When deps available, run fallback in draft mode.

## Logging
- Capture per-step logs (check/build/release) and manifest output.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and build logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fallback completes end-to-end when deps available.
- [ ] Partial failure surfaces clear error and non-zero exit.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:04.180185543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:47.456430053Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20l","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:41.812041864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20l","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:43.444477715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20l","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:45.590371481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20l","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:47.455571185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-271","title":"Unit tests for config.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for config.sh Module

## Module Overview
config.sh provides XDG-compliant configuration management:
- `config_init` - Create default config files
- `config_load` - Load config into DSR_CONFIG array
- `config_get` - Retrieve config values with defaults
- `config_set` - Update config values
- `config_validate` - Schema validation
- `config_show` - Display configuration

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Initialization Tests
- `config_init` creates all required directories
- Creates default config.yaml with valid schema
- Creates hosts.yaml with default hosts
- Creates repos.yaml with empty registry
- Creates secrets/ directory with proper permissions (700)
- Does NOT overwrite existing config (unless --force)
- --force flag recreates config from scratch

### 2. Loading Tests
- `config_load` populates DSR_CONFIG associative array
- Missing config file triggers error with helpful message
- Malformed YAML produces clear error
- Default values applied for missing keys

### 3. Get/Set Tests
- `config_get` returns correct values
- `config_get` with default returns default when key missing
- `config_set` updates in-memory value
- `config_set --persist` writes to disk
- Nested key access (e.g., `signing.enabled`)

### 4. Validation Tests
- Valid config passes validation
- Invalid schema_version fails
- Missing required fields fail
- Type mismatches detected (string vs int)

### 5. XDG Compliance Tests
- Respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME override
- Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME override
- Respects XDG_STATE_HOME override
- Falls back to ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/state

## Test Approach
- Use TEMP_DIR for isolated testing
- Set DSR_CONFIG_DIR to temp directory
- Clean up after each test
- NO mocking - test real file operations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:36:05.370272393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-271","title":"Unit tests for config.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for config.sh Module

## Module Overview
config.sh provides XDG-compliant configuration management:
- `config_init` - Create default config files
- `config_load` - Load config into DSR_CONFIG array
- `config_get` - Retrieve config values with defaults
- `config_set` - Update config values
- `config_validate` - Schema validation
- `config_show` - Display configuration

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Initialization Tests
- `config_init` creates all required directories
- Creates default config.yaml with valid schema
- Creates hosts.yaml with default hosts
- Creates repos.yaml with empty registry
- Creates secrets/ directory with proper permissions (700)
- Does NOT overwrite existing config (unless --force)
- --force flag recreates config from scratch

### 2. Loading Tests
- `config_load` populates DSR_CONFIG associative array
- Missing config file triggers error with helpful message
- Malformed YAML produces clear error
- Default values applied for missing keys

### 3. Get/Set Tests
- `config_get` returns correct values
- `config_get` with default returns default when key missing
- `config_set` updates in-memory value
- `config_set --persist` writes to disk
- Nested key access (e.g., `signing.enabled`)

### 4. Validation Tests
- Valid config passes validation
- Invalid schema_version fails
- Missing required fields fail
- Type mismatches detected (string vs int)

### 5. XDG Compliance Tests
- Respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME override
- Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME override
- Respects XDG_STATE_HOME override
- Falls back to ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/state

## Test Approach
- Use TEMP_DIR for isolated testing
- Set DSR_CONFIG_DIR to temp directory
- Clean up after each test
- NO mocking - test real file operations","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:07:50.479994517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-283","title":"E2E: dsr status (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr status (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify status output with real data sources (logs, cache, repos config) and JSON schema validity.

## Approach
- Run `dsr status` in a temp XDG state with seeded log files.
- If state is empty, ensure output is graceful and schema-valid.

## Logging
- Emit the temp XDG paths used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and relevant files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Human output includes last run summary when available.
- [ ] --json output matches schema and has empty stderr on success.
- [ ] Empty state handled without errors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:22.561427223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:29.840583523Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-283","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:28.014177245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-283","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:29.839327817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-29b","title":"E2E: dsr build (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr build (real behavior)

## Purpose
Exercise build command paths without mocks, using real local repos and dry-run/plan modes when external deps are missing.

## Approach
- Use a temp repo with a minimal workflow file for act.
- If docker/act missing, run `--dry-run` and assert planned actions.
- If docker/act available, execute a tiny workflow and verify artifacts.

## Logging
- Record repo path, workflow file, and tool config.
- On failure, dump act logs and stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] Real run (when deps available) produces artifacts + manifest.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:43.935502571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:37.024551685Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:35.186637406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:37.023320756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kf","title":"E2E: dsr repos (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr repos (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate repos subcommands using real repos.d config files without mocks.

## Scope
- `dsr repos list`
- `dsr repos info <tool>`
- `dsr repos validate`
- `dsr repos discover --dry-run`

## Approach
- Use temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME with a minimal repos.yaml + repos.d fixtures.
- Validate that list/info output matches fixtures.

## Logging
- Print fixture paths and command lines.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and repo files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All subcommands work with real files.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid and stderr clean on success.
- [ ] Errors on malformed YAML are clear and actionable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:32.489262223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:33.404083786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kf","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:31.579405945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kf","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:33.403724219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
